close_reason:
RESOLVED. Identified via debug-symbol macFUSE build + buck2 stress: __stack_chk_fail in libfuse 3.18.2 was open_auto_cache (fuse.c:4096) — 144B struct stat allocated on stack, our 192B fuse_darwin_attr write overran it by 48B. A second instance is hidden_name (fuse.c:2795) with the same pattern. Two-helper bug; both have the same fix.

Patches saved at nix/patches/macfuse-libfuse3/0001-darwin-attr-overflow-fix.patch — gate the fuse_fs_getattr call on fuse_fs_darwin_extensions_enabled() and use fuse_fs_getattr$DARWIN with a fuse_darwin_attr buffer when set. Symmetric to existing fuse_lib_getattr$DARWIN / fuse_lib_setattr$DARWIN splits.

Verified: with the patched libfuse loaded via DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH, the daemon survives ~12,000 sequential FUSE ops during a buck2 build without hitting the canary. Previous crash threshold was ~9,400 ops. Daemon now exits CLEANLY via fuse_destroy when something else (FSKit-side, separate issue) unmounts the volume.

Followups still on the path to a green turnkey-4vl.4 benchmark:

  * (small) File this fix upstream against macfuse/macfuse with the
    analysis. The patch is ~80 lines and isolated to libfuse3.
  * (medium) Package the patched libfuse via Nix so we don't need
    DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH at runtime. The macFUSE source is a meson build
    pulled from github.com/macfuse/macfuse; can apply
    nix/patches/macfuse-libfuse3/*.patch in a derivation, ship the
    resulting libfuse3.4.dylib alongside turnkey-composed.
  * (separate, blocking) FSKit force-unmounts the volume after ~10K-12K
    ops in a buck2 build. Daemon exits cleanly via fuse_destroy. Buck2
    sees EIO on whatever syscall it was making at the moment of unmount
    (almost always a symlink, since that's the bulk of materializer
    traffic). Likely an FSKit appex memory limit or some unhandled-op
    pattern. Need to reduce nodeid retention (728 FORGETs vs 11K
    LOOKUPs) or open an upstream macFUSE issue. New bead.
description:
Reproducible: with our daemon mounted via -o backend=fskit on macOS 26.4.1 + macFUSE 5.2.0, running 'buck2 build root//src/rust/composition:composition' from inside the mount kills the daemon within ~2.2 seconds.

CRASH SIGNATURE
  signal: SIGABRT (via __pthread_kill <- pthread_kill <- abort <- __stack_chk_fail)
  faulting frame: libfuse3.4.dylib offset 0xcb60 (lr after bl ___stack_chk_fail)
  parent frame:   libfuse3.4.dylib offset 0x195b0
  grandparent:    libfuse3.4.dylib fuse_session_process_buf_internal (0x177d0)

The function whose canary trips is at libfuse offset 0xc040..0xcb5c (~2.8 KB). Disassembly shows it takes 3 register args (req, ino, fi) — so x0=req, x1=ino, x2=fi — calls _fuse_reply_open and _fuse_reply_err but NOT _fuse_fs_unlink (that call I'd seen earlier was in the previous function in the binary at 0xbf10..0xc03c). Stack frame 0xb0 = 176 bytes. Best match: fuse_lib_open. lib_open is the same impl on Apple and non-Apple per the fuse_path_ops table (line 5862, no #ifdef around .open).

NEW: single-threaded does NOT fix it. With fuse_loop_mt_312 + max_threads=1 + idle=1, daemon still crashes after ~9400 ops processed sequentially. So this is NOT a concurrency bug — it's a deterministic buffer overflow that triggers after enough ops. Op-count breakdown of one such crash:

  GETATTR  2934
  LOOKUP   2091
  STATFS   1020
  MKDIR     796
  SETATTR   541
  RELEASE   411
  FORGET    290
  OPEN      284

The 15 worker threads in the crash dump are not libfuse workers (max_threads=1 limits those) — likely macFUSE FSKit shim / MFMount internal threads.

What we tried that did NOT help:
  * single-threaded fuse_loop_mt_312 max_threads=1
  * dropping the 'local' mount option
  * hardening fuse_readlink against size=0
  * adding a no-op setattr stub

Next things to try (further work, not done in this session):
  * Build a debug-symbol macFUSE libfuse so the crashing function symbolizes — without symbols we can only narrow it to 'a function that looks like fuse_lib_open' from disassembly heuristics.
  * Instrument every callback we register to log path + arg lengths, find the immediate-pre-crash op.
  * Replace each of our callbacks with an empty/ENOSYS stub one at a time and bisect which op pattern triggers the overflow.
  * Build a minimal C-only libfuse program (mkdir + symlink + chmod spam) and see if it crashes too, isolating whether the bug is in libfuse 3.18 itself or in our Rust↔C boundary.
  * Pin op_size = sizeof(fuse_operations) - 8 etc. (or various sizes) to see if the trailing-field offsets matter.
  * File upstream against macFUSE.

Until fixed, this blocks turnkey-4vl.4 (the cold-buck2-build benchmark).
title: macFUSE FSKit libfuse stack overflow under concurrent load (heavy buck2)
